title: Phlegmariurus schlechteri
taxonomy: Phlegmariurus schlechteri is a species of plant in the family Lycopodiaceae. It belongs to the class Lycopodiopsida, an order of vascular plants. Further classification places Phlegmariurus schlechteri within the order Hymnales, which is a group of lycopods. At the rank of family, it is placed within the family Lycopodiaceae, and at the rank of genus within the genus Phlegmariurus. The plant's classification provides insights into its evolutionary relationships with other lycopods.

intro: Phlegmariurus schlechteri, also known as the Schlechter's climbing fork fern, is a species of climbing fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Phlegmariurus schlechteri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Phlegmariurus schlechteri is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, often in combination with other medicinal plants, to treat respiratory conditions and other health issues.
